name: Soy Sesame Chicken with Oriental Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Hot chili paste with garlic
12 ounces Small carrots, thinly sliced diagonally
12 ounces Jicama, peeled and cut into thin matchstick size slices
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the first 9 ingredients.
2. Pour half of the marinade into a shallow dish and reserve the remaining for vegetables. Add the chicken to the marinade in the dish and turn to coat. Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours and up to 4 hours.
3. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Add carrots and simmer for 3 minutes. Add jicama and peas and cook until just tender, about 2 minutes. Drain.
4. Refresh vegetables with cold water and drain. (Can be prepared up to 4 hours ahead; cover and chill.)
5. Remove chicken from the marinade and heat oil in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken, skin side down, and cook for 3 minutes. Turn chicken and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Cook until chicken is cooked through, about 3 minutes.
6. Transfer chicken to plates and keep warm. Add broth and reserved marinade to the skillet. Add carrots, jicama, and snow peas and stir until heated through, about 3 minutes. Remove vegetables from heat.
7. Top chicken with vegetables and drizzle juices over.
